CurrentIssues.ID: ForbiddenComment:ManageTagsPage.kt$// TODO: when trying to delete a tag that is linked to projects, let the backend return that it is not possible (with a number associated of the linked projects)
MaxLineLength:ManageTagsPage.kt$// 1. Backend enforces it (source of truth): reject DELETE /tags/{id} if linked projects exist. ┃
MaxLineLength:ManageTagsPage.kt$// 1. Prevents accidental data integrity loss. ┃
MaxLineLength:ManageTagsPage.kt$// 2. Forces explicit user intent (detach/reassign first). ┃
MaxLineLength:ManageTagsPage.kt$// 2. Frontend surfaces it nicely: disable or warn in dialog (“Tag is used by X projects; remove links first”). ┃
MaxLineLength:ManageTagsPage.kt$// 3. Keeps history and reporting stable. ┃
MaxLineLength:ManageTagsPage.kt$// 3. Optional advanced flow later: “Delete and remove from all projects” as a separate destructive action. ┃
MaxLineLength:ManageTagsPage.kt$// Why this is usually best: ┃
